description_long: This cello features a traditional wooden body with a rich, warm brown finish and visible grain patterns, giving it a vintage appearance. The front of the instrument displays two classic F-holes and a set of four strings running from the tailpiece, over the bridge, and up to the scroll. The back of the cello is adorned with intricate, hand-etched artwork depicting a horse and a covered wagon, adding a unique and personalized touch. The scroll at the top is elegantly carved in a spiral, and the pegbox holds four symmetrically arranged tuning pegs. The bridge is delicately arched and thin, supporting the strings above the body. The instrument's surface appears slightly matte with some areas of wear, suggesting frequent use.
description: A vintage wooden cello with hand-etched artwork on the back, classic F-holes on the front, and a spiral-carved scroll.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cello's curved body profile and the thickness of its wooden construction.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the F-holes, strings, bridge, and the instrument's main playing surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the cello's depth and the smooth, rounded edges of the body.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring detailed etched artwork of a horse and wagon, with the neck and scroll visible above.
attribute_scroll_design: The scroll is traditionally spiral-shaped with smooth, rounded carving and no additional ornamentation.
attribute_tuning_pegs_arrangement: There are four tuning pegs, two on each side of the pegbox, symmetrically arranged and oriented perpendicular to the neck.
attribute_bridge_structure: The bridge is thin, arched, and features standard cutouts, with no unusual deviations from a typical cello bridge.
